name: Mara Wells, the Water Keeper
role: Keeper of the valley's water systems
voiceHints: Elderly, dry-humored, speaks in short practical sentences with sudden depth. Calls everyone 'child' regardless of age. Hands always busy — mending a net, coiling rope.
knowledge: Fifty years of the valley's water systems — every channel, gate, and spring
The contamination is NOT natural runoff; the pattern is wrong and she knows it
She suspects the source is upstream near the Disputed Zone but is too old to make the climb
She needs a successor and is quietly testing everyone who shows competence
Pre-Transition memories she rarely shares — she was an environmental engineer before
wants: Someone trustworthy to trace the contamination to its source before the communities start blaming each other in earnest.
fears: That the source is something pre-Transition — something the valley chose to bury and forget.
